title: Registered Nurse (RN)
summary: Provide direct patient care, coordinate treatment plans, educate patients and families, and often specialize by setting (ED, ICU, med-surg, ambulatory).
education: ADN or BSN + NCLEX-RN; many employers prefer BSN.
typical_shadowing: Floor nursing, charge nurse workflows, patient education, medication administration observation (no hands-on unless authorized).
outlook_note: Strong demand nationally with local variation; shift work common.

title: Physician Assistant / Associate (PA)
summary: Practice medicine on care teams: history, exam, orders, procedures within scope, often with supervising/collaborating physicians depending on state.
education: Master's PA program + PANCE; clinical rotations across specialties.
typical_shadowing: Primary care, surgery, EM, or specialty clinics; observe H&P and procedures.

title: Physician (MD/DO)
summary: Diagnose and treat illness, lead care teams, pursue residency specialty training after medical school.
typical_shadowing: 50–100+ hours common for applicants; specialty exploration across inpatient and outpatient.
outlook_note: Workforce shortages vary by specialty and geography.

title: Physical Therapist (PT)
summary: Restore movement and function through evaluation, exercise, manual therapy, and patient education.
typical_shadowing: Outpatient ortho, inpatient rehab, sports medicine observation hours often required for DPT apps.

title: Occupational Therapist (OT)
summary: Help people participate in daily activities (ADLs, work, school) after injury, illness, or disability.
education: Master's or OTD + NBCOT + state license.
typical_shadowing: Pediatrics, hand therapy, inpatient rehab, school-based OT.
outlook_note: Diverse settings; observation hours often expected.

title: Pharmacist
summary: Medication experts: dispensing, counseling, clinical pharmacy services, and medication safety.
education: PharmD + licensure (NAPLEX/MPJE); residencies for clinical tracks.
typical_shadowing: Community pharmacy, hospital pharmacy, ambulatory care clinics.
outlook_note: Roles expanding in clinical services and specialty pharmacy.

title: Emergency Medicine (physician specialty)
summary: Acute undifferentiated care: resuscitation, procedures, rapid decision-making across all ages.
typical_shadowing: ED observation with attending/resident; high acuity, shift-based.
outlook_note: Lifestyle and acuity differ sharply from clinic-based specialties.

title: Family Medicine (physician specialty)
summary: Longitudinal primary care for all ages; prevention, chronic disease, continuity relationships.
typical_shadowing: Outpatient clinic sessions; procedures and community health optional.
outlook_note: Critical to addressing primary care shortages.

title: Radiologic Technologist
summary: Perform diagnostic imaging exams (X-ray, CT, MRI tracks) with attention to safety and patient positioning.
typical_shadowing: Imaging department observation; radiation safety orientation required at many sites.
outlook_note: Stable allied-health pathway with specialty modalities.

title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
summary: Assist patients with ADLs, vital signs, and mobility under nursing supervision — common early clinical exposure role.
typical_shadowing: Often paid work rather than unpaid shadowing; strong patient-facing hours.
outlook_note: Entry pathway into nursing and allied careers.
